So in business there's two types of clients. There's the right clients and there's wrong clients And they are two completely different types of people but sometimes like Millions like little jungle people hiding off in the forest You can't see them and you can't tell the difference between who's who and it sucks because they're like a hidden bomb They're sneaking your business They trojan horse you and they just fuck everything up and what you don't understand is is that you can prevent Trojan horse from coming into your operation You can identify that it's a trojan horse not the gift of revenue for you when you're in those crises moments I'm gonna tell you what happens first of all just for what type of person I'm talking about So we can identify like any scenarios where you're gonna run into these people versus these people Okay, the wrong clients are the people who are the people who complain all the time Where the people who are blowing you up or the people who literally in your brain You know are not paying you the right amount and you don't feel good about just doing work for them Because you actually feel like and know that you are undervalued in that specific deal If your team is bitching about working with them too much and it's actually not a problematic client It's just that they just like they're bad person like the wrong client. You got you got to listen to your team sometimes You understand that the bad wrong client they do so much damage, but they give you revenue So you think they're good That is where we slip and get trojan horse ladies and gentlemen the wrong type of client to the people who come into your business Who at first seem like they're gonna be a workable deal, right? You go through your normal processes. Everything's cool. I'm used one there might be a heightened communication Meaning you have a higher frequency of communication with this type of client another client. Sometimes it's good. Sometimes it's bad Now that's an early identifier things might be wrong. However, let's keep going now You start delivering work for them and on the first thing that you show them for approval They make so many changes they list off That means you have a communication issue right off the rim as soon as you identify that you have a communication issue That's typically when you identify the whole series of problems coming your way You now got the wrong client on your hands as soon as you have a communication issue That's that's what life is. That's what your relationship with your client is at the end of the day It's just a bunch of communication cycles if those are not fluent and in Frequency and in alignment with what you want out of the deal. You got the wrong deal You got the wrong client some people they have a problem of firing clients We're gonna talk more about that in a second right now I need you to understand that as soon as you identify you have the wrong client and you keep digging the hole You are now responsible for fucking your business up. It's no longer the wrong client spot However, you still haven't identified at this point that somebody who's in this high of frequency of communication with you and is Sending everything back that you're trying to approve with them and you still think that they're the right kind of client Let's keep going. Let's keep going on the path. Okay, so now you've done every approval when you've gone back And they finally after maybe 10 or so changes like whatever you've done for them now you go to launch the product Okay, here's where things get really fun If you launch whatever it is for advertising as a digital agency to represent this person that you have taken on as a client That you haven't identified as the wrong client still you start doing marketing. This is almost like This is the red zone. This is like this is critical zone that you're about to enter into here You still haven't identified this so I really want you to focus on this part first Everything before this is when you want to identify the wrong client and fire them everything after this is where you kind of open yourself up for some extreme Liabilities because if you have the wrong client now as soon as you start advertising this person is going to be blowing you up This person is going to be hitting up every person within your company. They're going to be very Insecure and be very fearful. They're gonna have very inward personalities They're gonna almost battle you like an enemy instead of talking to you in cooperation about what you can do to work towards a result Even the best clients who do call you all the time will be identified as a positive good client based on the communication Type they have as long as there's a positive communication cycle inside negative And it's in the false positive you have the wrong client and you got to get him off your hand This person is gonna be blowing you up like oh, we're never gonna get results. Oh, that's not working. This is working change that I think you need to do this. Oh, I can't believe I paid this amount for all this stuff We're wasting money. I add oh my god and everything is gonna be a problem You as a business owner Probably being a young business owner that are gonna be running around with your head caught up as well Trying to put out all the fires you are gonna become captain America put out fires all over the place because that's your job Is not your computer sir. You have your fire. I was putting all your attention everywhere in your company just Dealing with problematic wrong client Here's the thing nothing actually wrong everything's right. I'm sure that you're actually doing a great job Most cases sometimes I'm wrong here. Sometimes you're actually not bad job Sometimes a good person becomes wrong client because sometimes you do you do mess up I can go that way wrong client once again is gonna be having you put out fires everywhere your time energy All your company resources are gonna go all into this one client all of a sudden what you're gonna typically find now at this point Your cycle okay, you still haven't identified this is the wrong client Which you now should because you're learning the hard way instead of learning and firing the person from an identifying way That's the difference in that point. The wrong client is now showed you that the wrong client Here's what things get worse You are now probably talking to the client in desperation trying to calm the person down I'm trying to just shut them up and you're talking about it with your team and you're just all stressed out now You get on the phone with the person they're just freaking out too and they sound disappointed almost like like an upset parent of some kind And then when you finally get them almost calm what happens? The worst thing they say either one or two things They want a refund or they're gonna leave a bad review or worse. They're gonna do both Now here's the thing about the wrong client. They're probably also one of your lowest paying clients you've ever had That's the worst part it's gonna make you sick on the inside you're gonna be like oh my gosh Jeremy that one time and he told me about this kind of client. I remember now This is the person who's my lowest paying client ever who's gonna leave me a bad review Who's gonna go tell all our friends that I'm a scam even though in reality I did everything I was supposed to and then they ended up not doing what they need to do and now I'm here Because you didn't identify the wrong client. Let me tell you this I've had the wrong client for about six months now I've had all the right clients if I've had any clients that have gone away They haven't been bad clients They've just been people that the relationship hasn't worked out and we've separated ways on a logical consensus that just wasn't working The right client will do that in a separation the wrong client will leave in a fury and go trash your business The wrong client can be identified fired and let go here is how you get stuck with the wrong client I'm gonna relate to you right now, and I'm sure that no matter where you're at the business You've probably been able to identify with this circumstance We know the wrong client as soon as they present themselves to us. I know that you know It's not hard to tell soon as you get a deal in front of you even if they're hidden in the forest You can typically see that right? You know that they're the wrong deal, but you typically take them on because you're in a crisis financially And I don't mean that your business is gonna fall apart, but it feels like it Maybe you have some bills to pay off you have payroll coming up or you're going on a vacation this weekend Or you want to go get some new shoes or whatever the fuck you think you're gonna pay for that You're justifying take me on this wrong deal with here's what typically happens You take on the wrong deal you pay off whatever you need to pay off You get the bills covered you pay the expenses you fund your payroll or whatever you do and you're glad it's next you Immediately feel more supported you took on the wrong deal, but your bank account is dwindling back down to zero So you're considering going out and taking on another wrong deal Now if you manage to take on enough out of the wrong client and you end up with a little bit left over right? Here's the problem when the wrong client does present themselves to you and you have identified as a point where you need to fire them before The critical zone happens You don't have any money left in order to refund them because you've already spent that client's money on whatever expenses and bills you're putting out It's not familiar The wrong client we know they are You need to develop a standard in your business to not take on the wrong client ever if you ever take on the wrong client You know that you're opening yourself up to liabilities The hard part about this kind of lesson that once you see you can't unsee You will now know every wrong client that presents themselves to you and you will know by the identifiers of high communication Unsettled and unsatisfactory communication just in the very first few days of your cycles together as a client or anything's even happened And even worse when you reach the critical zone where they get to a point where they're about to enter that by going through a bunch of Hey, I'm like all this stuff. I want to change it all kind of thing and then when you actually start taking action That's when it gets bad and that's what you open yourself up for liability and I'm asking for returns or refunds at which point you can't do anything Now there's a whole other video of the products and services category on no refunds And you are going to be able to legally cover yourself to protect yourself against this individual However, what you're gonna have to understand is dependent on the price that you pay depends on the actual court system You're going to be able to do it in a civil case in case that you ever do this soon I've never been sued because my agreements are bulletproof and you actually can't take me to court when you sign an agreement with me However, you have to understand you still have to be ethically aligned to make sure that you're delivering on whatever it is That you're signing people up for because in the agreement it does list what you say You're actually going to deliver to the individual and if you don't deliver those things to the individual or get the wrong client To sign off wink wink what we're about to talk about as the advanced tip on every milestone that you're accomplishing You're not going to protect yourself. We're going to open yourself up for liability The wrong client needs to go through an advanced process You're going to be able to download below called a milestone agreement. Okay, so with the wrong client as soon as you identify them I'm just going to give you the worst case scenario here just in case you already have the wrong client Okay, I'm not advocating you taking on wrong clients This is not a wrong client hack in order for you to go out and get wrong clients and feel good about it This is for if you're in a problematic scenario where your bank accounts turns to dwindle and you do find yourself Considering taking on the wrong client and you make the unfortunate decision to actually do it This is what you're going to do. Okay, first of all with any client You have them signing an agreement for what you're going to do in the agreement clearly defined Not in a technical vague way within a very specific action item way One through however many steps you're going to accomplish in whatever the timetable is typically the wrong client will only sign with you for that First 30 days, and they won't be with you after that. So build it out for a 30-day window. That's your time to list off the Exact actions that you are going to take step by step that you are going to call milestones That you are going to accomplish. They're literally very clear listed actions not vague very specific like number one We are going to revamp. I wouldn't even use the word revamp actually. I don't need that statement I would say number one we are going to create cover photos for the Facebook LinkedIn and Twitter pages of Person X and here are the links I would then say we are going to create X amount of graphic images and X amount of graphic videos or whatever We're gonna post on these channels on these date ranges Boom, I would then say we are going to report analytics on these days and from this tool and here's an example report attached to this I would then say we're gonna create a landing page in a funnel and the landing page in funnel is going to be under two Specific revision timetables where the person at client X can revise two times and cannot go past that and then like boom That's it and I would say client will sign Milestone agreements for each step and after milestone agreements are accomplished The cycle is closed and the window for change is closed and the agreement is one out of X amount of steps closer To finalization is what you want to say client is fully aware and acknowledges All the legal terminology in the agreement will be able to download below That says you are done with this step and you do not open yourself up for liability You literally close the opportunity for liability here. It's incredible if you do take on the wrong client You're gonna do these milestone agreements so each time you accomplish one through whatever you have the person to sign off that it's done Even if they're unsatisfied it doesn't matter after you've actually finished that step You have to have a spot in your agreement legally Which is in the in the little download section below if you're watching the DAP videos right now where you actually say in the agreement if the client does not approve the Milestone agreement after two days and no more revisions are allowed The client is forfeiting his ability to do anything legally in this agreement and forfeits the agreement and the agreement is Terminated so like you actually tell the person that if they don't sign the milestone agreement that the agreement gets Terminated and then you don't have to legally accomplish anything else. Let's go over to the right client. The right client is gonna be somebody who's very Just goes right. It's a very synergistic relationship. You know, there's no there's no turmoil. There's no beef You know, there's nothing wrong. Everything's right. You know, you're having fluency in your creative process You're giving the person something to approve. They're like, that's awesome. You know, you're doing ads or taking action They're loving it. They're like it's all praising the warrior on both sides. It's like, hey, we love you That's the right kind of client the right client when you're selling them things go really smooth Things are really fluent. It's like you have this and I have this and it's like, oh my gosh This is live pops and rainbows like we should have a relationship together in business Like how do we not know each other yet? And you sign an agreement the same day and the money's there and like they have money for ads and you're you just in a perfect Relationship feels great as a business owner. You literally are like wow This is this is perfect. They're called the right clients. They come in high frequency You just have to know your right client is I've been able to identify the majority of the right clients in the marketplace No matter what industry you're in or the people who are not financially struggling. Here's the trick DAP student 97% of the world is struggling only 3% of the world lives above the 400k poverty line That's serious 3% of the market is going to be able to be the right type of client for you if you're like me That being said I find these people collect commonly It's all about your position you go back to phase number two If you don't understand positioning to be able to understand how to connect yourself in entire markets all through Where you position yourself? What are you speak? How you speaking what you're doing? style of modern 2017 age for entrepreneurship 23-year-old on top of that they give me everywhere I go for who I associate with and on top of that a reverse engineer losing the 3% what they're doing how they're addressing what they're wearing and just overall their attitudes morale's events places they go places hanging out foods they eat you do the same things you got to repeat successful actions And associate with what you want to be and how you want to be Be able to reverse engineer where you write peppercorn is and figure out how you can beat there go to the revents We'll talk more about sales portion of this But now I just want you to understand when you're selling your products and services more importantly the right type of client And even though the wrong type of client might be attracted to this too the right type of client Just just looks at your product and service and it just makes perfect sense That's how you know that you came up with the right product and service when you're attracting the right clients If you are only attracting the wrong clients one or two things you're in the wrong market Or your product and service isn't valuable enough to attract the right client for what you're selling as a digital agency Or to understand this there's people just like me just like me But a thousand of me that are crushing it selling the right clients every day because they're in the right markets They have the right product They have the right service that aligns with the right person most people have the same products and services But they don't align it the right way they don't position themselves the right ways they get the wrong client They just think that's the type of business and that's the type of people that are coming to them as clients in this type of business And they have a skewed perception of reality You can control what type of people you work with ladies and gentlemen You only have to make sure that your perspective clients see the value in your products and services That's how you attract the right people anyway, remember this ladies and gentlemen If you got the right package you can get the right clients if you have the wrong clients Use the milestone agreements lock them down strong arm them so they can't strong arm you Make sure you protect yourself and make sure you fire the people and save a little bit of your cheddar So you can just refund them their money back or refund them a part of their money back If you get stuck in the deal milestone agreements all day Preferably though to have a business full of the right clients.
